records.stage_evidence: Radiopaque catheter visible in pelvicalyceal system
records.summary: An abdominal AP (KUB) X-ray showing a radiopaque catheter in the pelvicalyceal system, consistent with post-procedural imaging. The patient is a 25-year-old male, imaged supine on 4/3/2026.
key_observations: A radiopaque catheter is visible extending from the upper abdomen into the pelvis.
Spine and pelvic bones are clearly visualized.
Image is labeled 'ABDOMEN AP[KUB]' and patient details '25Y/M ABDOMEN, SUPINE 4/3/2026'.

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for an X-ray KUB (Kidney, Ureter, Bladder) examination. The scan was compromised due to poor bowel preparation. A DJ stent is visible on the left side, but no definite radio-opaque shadow is seen in the KUB region. The vertebrae appear unremarkable. The report is signed by a Consultant Radiologist.
key_observations: Scan compromised due to poor bowel preparation
DJ stent seen on left side
No definite radio-opaque shadow in KUB region

records.stage_evidence: Report of a completed IVP study

ocr.verbatim:
PLAIN X-RAY : Shows normal psoas and kidney shadows.
A radio-opaque calculus is seen in right renal area at the level of L2 vertebral body measuring 3.1mm
Few faint radio-opaque calculi are seen in left renal area at the level of L1 and L2 vertebral bodies with largest measuring 3.4mm.
A radio-opaque calculi are seen in left paravertebral area at the level of L2-L3 vertebral bodies measuring 6.7mm with DJ stent is seen in situ on left side.
Visualized spine appears normal.

INTRA-VEINOUS UROGRAPHY :
IVU done with inj. Omnipaque 300mg/ml x50ml IV.
Both kidneys are normally sited and are of normal size and shape. Kidneys measure 102x50mm and 100x50mm or right and left sides respectively.
There is normal excretion of contrast on both sides.

Right kidney:
Pelviccalyceal system is normal on right side. A radio-opaque calculus in right renal area is seen to lie inferior calyx s/o right renal calculus. Right ureter is normal in course and calibre. There is no hold up of contrast on right side.

Left kidney:
Few faint radio-opaque calculi seen in left renal area are seen to lie in left middle and inferior calyces. A radio-opaque calculus noted in left paravertebral location are seen to lie in left upper ureter with DJ stent is seen in situ on left side.

Urinary bladder shows smooth outline. No filling defect or calculus is identified in bladder. No significant residual urine is seen after micturition.

IMPRESSION : IVU study reveals:
* Right renal calculus
* Left renal calculi and left upper ureteric calculus with Dj stent seen in situ on left side.
* Normal excretion of contrast in bilateral kidney.

ADV: Clinical correlation and further evaluation with nuclear medicine scan.

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for an Intravenous Urography (IVP) study. It details findings of renal calculi (kidney stones) in both kidneys, a DJ stent in the left ureter, and normal kidney function and anatomy. The report is dated March 31, 2026.
key_observations: Left renal calculi and left upper ureteric calculus with DJ stent in situ on left side
Normal excretion of contrast in both kidneys

records.summary: A photograph of a medical monitor displaying six sequential IVP (Intravenous Pyelogram) radiographs of the abdomen, showing the progression of contrast through the urinary tract over time, from 'Plan' to 'Post Void'.
key_observations: Six IVP images arranged in a 2x3 grid on a monitor
Images labeled: 'Plan', '5 Min', '10 Min', '15 Min', 'Full Bladder', 'Post Void'
Patient identifier 'L.V.P.' and date '31/03/2028' visible at bottom
Contrast medium visible in renal pelvis and ureters in all frames
Images show the urinary tract from the lumbar spine to the pelvis

limitations: Photograph of a monitor, not the original radiograph
Image resolution limited by photograph quality
